The company has a subscribed capital of Birr 2.5 billion and paid-up capital of Birr [amount not specified in the excerpt].

root_nodes.title: 1.4 Billion;
root_nodes.summary: The company, registered as MT/AA/3/0035454/2008, is headquartered at Bahiru Abraham Commercial Tower, Addis Ababa, and will hold its 7th Annual Ordinary General Meeting on December 20, 2022, at the Addis Ababa Hilton. The meeting agenda includes approving share transfers, annual reports, auditors' fees, and board compensation, among other items. The company operates under the motto "Rising with Africa," reflecting its commitment to leveraging Africa's economic growth.

root_nodes.title: Establishment
root_nodes.summary: Ethio Re is the first reinsurance company incorporated under Article 5(8) of the Insurance Business Proclamation No. 746/2012, commencing operations on July 1, 2016 in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. The company provides both life and non-life reinsurance services, offering comprehensive inward reinsurance coverage to domestic insurers while also accepting international business selectively. Ethio Re aims to reduce foreign currency outflow by minimizing cross-border reinsurance transactions and enhancing local underwriting capacity and solvency.

root_nodes.title: Our Credit Rating
root_nodes.summary: Ethio-Re received its initial international and national scale strength ratings of AA (ET) from Global Credit Rating (GCR) with a stable outlook. The company also obtained a Financial Strength rating of B (Fair) and Long Term rating of bb (Fair) with stable outlook from AM Best rating agency, as part of its strategic plan 'Vision 2030'. These ratings were identified as one of the 'Big Five Pillars' of the company's newly designed strategic framework.

WHO WE ARE
root_nodes.summary: The document states that the mission is "to be the reinsurer of choice in Ethiopia and our chosen market." No additional mission, vision, or core values are provided in this section.

root_nodes.title: Core Values
root_nodes.summary: The Ethiopian Re management and staff use the mnemonic 'ETHIOPIAN' to represent their corporate mission and vision. The document indicates this approach with the notation "2" following the mnemonic.

root_nodes.title: Mission
root_nodes.summary: The company provides dependable reinsurance services locally and internationally by maintaining adequate risk capacity, employing highly trained professionals, and utilizing modern ICT systems. It aims to foster market stability, high professionalism, and advance the insurance business while emphasizing corporate social responsibility. The document includes six images without captions.

root_nodes.title: Strategic Partnerships
root_nodes.summary: Ethio-Re collaborates with key stakeholders to develop a resilient and forward-looking insurance ecosystem. The company works directly with these partners to shape the future of insurance in Ethiopia.

root_nodes.title: Market-leading insurance firms
root_nodes.summary: This document section states that the organization drives innovation, compliance, and sustainable growth across the sector. The text emphasizes a collaborative approach to achieving these objectives.

root_nodes.title: Training & Market Development
root_nodes.summary: Ethiopian Reinsurance S.C. (Ethio-Re) delivered over 18 specialized training programs in the 2024/25 financial year and hosts monthly capacity-building sessions to strengthen the insurance market through knowledge development. The company became a signatory member of the Nairobi Declaration on Sustainable Insurance in 2024 and aims to integrate sustainable insurance principles into its operations while advocating for ESG practices across the industry. Ethio-Re is committed to becoming an African ESG champion by allocating resources to sustainable projects and promoting environmental, social, and governance principles at Ethiopian, continental, and regional levels.

root_nodes.title: Ethio-Re discharging its Corporate Social Responsibility
root_nodes.summary: The document section describes the Ethiopian National Association of the Deaf (ENAD) school feeding program, which provides support for feeding programs at the CO-Action school for deaf children. It also mentions shareholders and ownership structures related to supporting feeding programs for needy individuals. The section appears to outline organizational and financial aspects of deaf education and community support initiatives in Ethiopia.

root_nodes.title: Tesfa- Birhan- Kirkos sub city
root_nodes.summary: The Ethiopian National Association of the Deaf (ENAD) operates a school feeding program for the CO-Action school for deaf children. Ethio Re's shareholding structure consists of partnerships between public and private investors, including seven banks, seventeen insurance companies, one hundred two individuals from various backgrounds, and one trade union. The company's subscribed share capital is Birr 2.5 Billion, and its Board of nine members is responsible for managing company affairs and implementing strategic directions.

root_nodes.title: Capital structure
root_nodes.summary: The legal minimum paid-up capital required to establish a reinsurance company in Ethiopia is Birr 500,000,000 (USD$ 10 million). Ethiopian Re's subscribed capital is Birr 2.5 billion (USD 50 million), which is significantly higher than the minimum requirement for a newly operational company.

root_nodes.title: UNDERWRITING CAPACITY
root_nodes.summary: Ethio-Re is a leading reinsurer in Ethiopia that also operates in African and selected Asian markets, offering reinsurance solutions to direct insurance companies. The company provides treaty and facultative reinsurance capacities across various classes of business, including Fire and Engineering, Marine Hull and Cargo, Casualty, Political Violence and Terrorism, Bond, and Aviation small Aircraft. Its reinsurance capacities range from $850,000 to $43,000,000 depending on the business class and type of coverage.
